commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1abac54087015d29b8ca14f266cc7ae77d695589)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a macOS update-mode early return before the running-gateway restart prompt and adds a regression test for that skip path.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +20. Total +23 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, not live: current main reaches the default-Yes running-gateway restart prompt when healthOk is false and serviceRuntime.status is running. I did not run a macOS LaunchAgent update reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor/maintainer handling for real macOS behavior proof and a decision on the broader post-update prompt coverage; no repair marker because contributor proof is still missing.

target.title: fix(doctor): skip gateway restart prompt during updates
target.body:
What does this PR do?
- Skips the macOS gateway restart prompt while `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS=1` and the gateway is already running.
- Adds a regression test that ensures the restart prompt is not reached in update mode.

Root cause
- The doctor flow still asked the runtime-repair prompt for a running gateway even during update-mode repairs, which can loop on restart verification.

Fix
- Return early from the running-service branch when the platform is macOS and `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS=1`.
- Add a test covering the skip path.

Why this shape
- Keeps normal doctor behavior unchanged outside update mode.
- Limits the fix to the update path that triggers the loop.

Tests
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.test.ts`

Related PRs / issues
- Fixes #86518
target.author: wesleysimplicio

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
